description: Audit-log fixture for the FORA-200.5 / FORA-401 idempotency spine. Captured by running the `claim()` primitive against a real Postgres 16 instance (fora-postgres) and the in-memory `AuditSink` collector. Confirms the FORA-200 plan §4 acceptance bar: 6 distinct `sync.{source,target}.{issue,comment,stage}.ok` event types emitted on the first claim; 0 events on the 9 subsequent replays of the same `(tenant_id, external_id, op_kind)`.

test_cases: returns true exactly once across 10x replays of the same key
emits all 6 sync.{source,target}.{ok,fail} event types on the first claim
emits ZERO audit events on 9 replays after the first claim
isolates claims per (tenant_id, external_id, op_kind) tuple
isolates claims per op_kind — same external_id, different op_kind is distinct
AuditSink throws → next replay retries the full claim (transactional rollback)

indexes: sync_op_pending_idx (tenant_id, claimed_at) WHERE outcome IS NULL
webhook_dedupe_pending_idx (tenant_id, received_at) WHERE processed_at IS NULL

acceptance_bar.migration_applies_cleanly: PASS — applied to dogfood Postgres 16 with no errors (DROP POLICY NOTICEs expected on first run)
acceptance_bar.ten_of_ten_tests_pass: PASS — 6/6 vitest cases in 119ms; 10x replay property holds (1 first_time + 9 replays)
acceptance_bar.six_audit_events_on_first_claim_zero_on_: PASS — 6 events on first claim (one of each closed set), 0 on the 9 replays

out_of_scope_notes: FORA-402 (issue mirror), FORA-404 (comment mirror), FORA-405 (workflow mapping) are children 1/2/3 — they consume this spine and add the per-op_kind fail-event emissions.
FORA-406 (divergence cron) reuses the `sync_op_pending_idx` for its nightly stuck-op sweep.
The `webhook_dedupe` table is created and RLS'd in this migration; FORA-200.6 (FORA-407 candidate) wires the inbound webhook handler to claim it on every Jira delivery.
